name: tui-presentation
description: TUI/chat presentation for agents: seven-shape vocabulary (framed card, sub-framed grid, ruled card, column-ruled, pipeline, state diagram, no-frame list), document structure with cordoning, and build discipline for alignment and overflow. Designed for rendering contexts where markdown does not render — Unicode box-drawing, whitespace, and alignment are the complete structural toolkit.

interface.shortDescription: Seven-shape vocabulary, document structure, and build discipline for monospace chat output
interface.longDescription: Produce readable, aligned Unicode presentations for design, architecture, comparisons, tradeoffs, UI mockups, data flows, state machines, and multi-section explanations in rendering contexts where markdown does not render. Teaches seven shapes, document structure with cordoning, alignment mechanics, and overflow policy. Strong user preference — reach for this whenever explaining, comparing, or proposing design in chat or TUI output.
